top_categories.workout_context: 11,811ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 104, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Dreiländergiro's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 104 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
114ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Dreiländergiro's paved Alpine mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 104 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Dreiländergiro. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 11,811ft of climbing on paved Alpine mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
At Dreiländergiro, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
104 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Alpine mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 104 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Dreiländergiro’s 104 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Dreiländergiro with 11,811ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Stelvio summit at 2,757m: wind chill can bring effective temperature near freezing even in June.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 104 miles of paved alpine mountain passes in Nauders am Reschenpass, Tyrol, Austria.
